name: extensions
context: PostgreSQL extensions: pgvector for embeddings, PostGIS for geospatial, pg_trgm for fuzzy search
info: PostgreSQL's extension system allows adding functionality without modifying the core database. pgvector adds vector similarity search for AI embeddings — store OpenAI, Cohere, or local model embeddings and query by cosine, L2, or inner product distance. PostGIS adds geospatial types and functions for location-based queries — points, polygons, distance calculations, and spatial indexing. pg_trgm enables trigram-based fuzzy text matching and similarity search. Other essential extensions include hstore for key-value storage, uuid-ossp for UUID generation, and built-in full-text search with tsvector and tsquery. Install extensions with `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS extension_name;`.
body:
# PostgreSQL Extensions

PostgreSQL's extension system is one of its greatest strengths. Extensions add data types, functions, operators, and index types without modifying the core database. Install any extension with `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS extension_name;`.

## pgvector

pgvector adds vector similarity search to PostgreSQL — essential for AI/ML applications that store and query embeddings.

```sql
-- Install pgvector
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;

-- Create a table with a vector column (1536 dimensions for OpenAI ada-002)
CREATE TABLE documents (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  content TEXT NOT NULL,
  embedding vector(1536)
);

-- Insert an embedding
INSERT INTO documents (content, embedding)
VALUES ('PostgreSQL is great', '[0.1, 0.2, 0.3, ...]'::vector);

-- Cosine similarity search (find nearest neighbors)
SELECT id, content, 1 - (embedding <=> $1::vector) AS similarity
FROM documents
ORDER BY embedding <=> $1::vector
LIMIT 10;

-- Create an HNSW index for fast approximate nearest neighbor search
CREATE INDEX ON documents USING hnsw (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
WITH (m = 16, ef_construction = 64);

-- Create an IVFFlat index (alternative, faster build, slightly less accurate)
CREATE INDEX ON documents USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
WITH (lists = 100);
```

Distance operators: `<=>` cosine distance, `<->` L2 (Euclidean) distance, `<#>` negative inner product.

## PostGIS

PostGIS adds geospatial types and functions for location-based applications.

```sql
-- Install PostGIS
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS postgis;

-- Create a table with a geography column
CREATE TABLE locations (
  id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  name TEXT NOT NULL,
  coords geography(POINT, 4326)
);

-- Insert a point (longitude, latitude)
INSERT INTO locations (name, coords)
VALUES ('Eiffel Tower', ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint(2.2945, 48.8584), 4326));

-- Find locations within 5km of a point
SELECT name, ST_Distance(coords, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint($1, $2), 4326)) AS distance_m
FROM locations
WHERE ST_DWithin(coords, ST_SetSRID(ST_MakePoint($1, $2), 4326), 5000)
ORDER BY distance_m;

-- Create a spatial index
CREATE INDEX ON locations USING GIST (coords);
```

## pg_trgm

pg_trgm provides trigram-based similarity matching for fuzzy text search.

```sql
-- Install pg_trgm
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm;

-- Fuzzy search with similarity threshold
SELECT name, similarity(name, 'postgre') AS sim
FROM products
WHERE similarity(name, 'postgre') > 0.3
ORDER BY sim DESC;

-- Use the % operator for similarity
SELECT * FROM products WHERE name % 'postgre';

-- GIN index for fast trigram searches
CREATE INDEX ON products USING GIN (name gin_trgm_ops);
```

## Full-Text Search

PostgreSQL has built-in full-text search without any extension:

```sql
-- Add tsvector column and index
ALTER TABLE articles ADD COLUMN search_vector tsvector;
UPDATE articles SET search_vector = to_tsvector('english', title || ' ' || body);
CREATE INDEX ON articles USING GIN (search_vector);

-- Search with ranking
SELECT title, ts_rank(search_vector, query) AS rank
FROM articles, to_tsquery('english', 'postgres & replication') AS query
WHERE search_vector @@ query
ORDER BY rank DESC;
```

## Installing Extensions

```sql
-- List available extensions
SELECT * FROM pg_available_extensions ORDER BY name;

-- List installed extensions
\dx
-- or
SELECT * FROM pg_extension;

-- Install
CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS extension_name;

-- Upgrade
ALTER EXTENSION extension_name UPDATE;

-- Remove
DROP EXTENSION extension_name;
```

| Extension | Purpose | Index Type |
|-----------|---------|------------|
| pgvector | Vector similarity search | HNSW, IVFFlat |
| PostGIS | Geospatial data and queries | GiST |
| pg_trgm | Trigram fuzzy matching | GIN, GiST |
| hstore | Key-value pairs in a column | GIN, GiST |
| uuid-ossp | UUID generation functions | B-tree |
| citext | Case-insensitive text type | B-tree |

sections.title: pgvector
sections.body: pgvector adds the `vector` data type and distance operators for similarity search. Use `<=>` for cosine distance, `<->` for L2, `<#>` for negative inner product. Create HNSW indexes for fast approximate nearest neighbor search. Typical use: store OpenAI embeddings (1536 dimensions) and query by similarity. See [[postgres/performance#index-strategies]] for index tuning.

sections.title: PostGIS
sections.body: PostGIS adds `geometry` and `geography` types for spatial data. Use `geography(POINT, 4326)` for lat/lon points. `ST_Distance()` computes distance in meters. `ST_DWithin()` filters by radius. `ST_Contains()`, `ST_Intersects()` for polygon operations. Always create a GiST index on spatial columns for performance.

sections.title: pg_trgm
sections.body: pg_trgm splits text into trigrams (three-character sequences) for fuzzy matching. The `similarity()` function returns a 0-1 score. The `%` operator filters by a configurable threshold (default 0.3). Create a GIN index with `gin_trgm_ops` for fast fuzzy searches. Useful for search-as-you-type and typo tolerance.

sections.title: Full-text search
sections.body: Built-in full-text search uses `tsvector` (document) and `tsquery` (search expression). The `@@` operator matches documents. `ts_rank()` scores relevance. Use `to_tsvector('english', text)` for language-aware stemming. Create a GIN index on the tsvector column for performance. Supports boolean operators: `&` (AND), `|` (OR), `!` (NOT), `<->` (phrase). See [[postgres/queries]] for integrating full-text search into queries.

sections.title: Installing extensions
sections.body: Install with `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS name;`. List available extensions with `pg_available_extensions` catalog. List installed with `\dx` in psql. Some extensions require the shared library to be present on the server (configured in `shared_preload_libraries` for pg_stat_statements, for example). Cloud-managed databases may restrict available extensions — check your provider's documentation.

links.context: Write queries that use extension types and operators
Index types for extension data (HNSW, GiST, GIN)
Verify extension indexes are used in query plans

related.note: pgvector stores OpenAI embeddings for vector similarity search
